digest.top_line: OLIGOMA: mPFS 35.8 vs 20.4mo (HR 0.48, p=0.021); first positive phase III for MDT in oligometastatic breast cancer.
digest.tldr: Breast and prostate carried ESTRO 2026. OLIGOMA delivers the first positive phase III for MDT in oligometastatic breast (HR 0.48); multiple 10-yr fractionation trials consolidate hypofractionation safety across the board. Prostate: PEACE-2 pelvic RT neutral in PSMA-staged era while RADIOSA adds the only randomized ADT + SBRT data in oligorecurrence (HR 0.39).

sites.intro: Five trials, two toxicity readouts and three efficacy signals, with pelvic nodal RT benefit now in question in the PSMA-staged long-ADT era.

studies.name: PACE-NODES
studies.tldr: PACE-NODES: pelvic nodal SBRT adds acute GI toxicity (28% vs 21% G≥2) over prostate-only SBRT; GU toxicity no different; efficacy data pending.
details: 🔍 Phase 3 RCT, N=1166, high-risk localised PCa (cT3a-T4 and/or Gleason 8-10 and/or PSA>20); 12-36mo ADT; P-SBRT 36.25Gy/5f vs PPN-SBRT 36.25Gy/5f prostate + 25Gy/5f nodes on alternate days
📊 Primary acute toxicity EPs: CTCAE G≥2 GI and GU up to 12wk; primary efficacy EP (time to biochemical/clinical failure) not yet mature
📊 G≥2 GI toxicity: 28% PPN-SBRT vs 21% P-SBRT over 12wk; no difference at 12wk endpoint (symptoms resolved quickly)
📊 G≥2 GU toxicity: no difference between arms (clinician or patient-reported)
📊 Patient-reported EPIC-26 bowel domain worse in PPN-SBRT at 4wk; GU domain similar
⚠️ 11% PPN-SBRT and 4% P-SBRT did not receive allocated treatment, mostly constraint violations -- higher non-delivery rate in nodal arm suggests technical complexity in multicentre rollout
🔗 vs POP-RT and PEACE-2 (conventional fractionation pelvic nodal RT): those trials showed bcFFS benefit; PACE-NODES will test whether SBRT fractionation replicates that -- cancer control data awaited

studies.name: RADIOSA
studies.tldr: RADIOSA post-hoc: SBRT + short-term ADT vs SBRT alone, median MFS 16.6mo vs not reached, HR 0.39 favoring combination.
details: 📊 Metastatic progression: 32/51 (62.7%) Arm A (SBRT) vs 19/51 (37.3%) Arm B (SBRT + ADT); median MFS 16.6mo (95% CI 12.83-NA) vs not reached
📐 HR 0.3894 (95% CI 0.2201-0.6888), p=0.00119; log-rank p=0.00079 — 61% risk reduction for metastatic progression
🔍 Phase II RCT, N=102, 1:1 randomization, oligorecurrent prostate cancer; median f/u 49.23mo (95% CI 42.47-54.8); post-hoc MFS/eugonadal MFS analysis
📊 Eugonadal MFS (after testosterone recovery) also significantly longer in Arm B (p<0.05); all but 2 Arm B pts recovered testosterone within f/u
⚠️ Post-hoc analysis of phase II trial — not powered for MFS; primary endpoint was biochemical PFS; eugonadal MFS is a novel non-validated endpoint
🔗 Aligns with ORIOLE/STOMP showing metastasis-directed therapy benefit in oligorecurrence, but RADIOSA is the only randomized data adding short-term ADT to SBRT in this setting
❓ Whether eugonadal MFS advantage translates to OS benefit and optimal ADT duration (6mo used here) remain open

studies.name: PIVOTALboost
studies.tldr: PIVOTALboost: focal boost + pelvic nodal RT (20 fx) safe at 2 yrs; early bowel toxicity with nodal RT resolves by 18 weeks.
details: 🔍 phase 3 RCT, N=1465, 39 UK centres; 3 arms: prostate IMRT alone (P), P+focal boost (P+B), P+pelvic nodes+boost (PPN+B); 20-fraction moderately hypofractionated; primary EP biochemical/clinical failure (not reported here)
📊 Late G3+ bowel side effects at 2 yrs: 8.2% (P), 8.7% (P+B), 6.5% (PPN+B) — no significant differences across arms
📊 Cumulative late G2+ side effects at 2 years (973 pts, 74% with ≥2yr f/u)
📊 Early bowel toxicity (≤18 wks): increased with pelvic node RT (PPN+B arm) vs prostate-only; no effect size reported in source tweets beyond visual curve separation
⚠️ Only secondary toxicity endpoints reported here; primary EP (biochemical/clinical failure) not yet presented — efficacy inference premature
🔗 Consistent with FLAME trial showing intraprostatic focal boost improves biochemical control without added late toxicity; PIVOTALboost extends this to 20-fx hypofractionation context
❓ Efficacy data (bFFS) pending; whether PPN+B arm translates early nodal RT toxicity spike into biochemical benefit vs P+B alone is the key unanswered question

studies.name: PEACE-2
studies.tldr: PEACE-2: pelvic RT did not significantly improve cPFS vs prostate-only RT (HR 0.81 [0.63-1.03], p=0.088) in very high-risk localized PCa.
details: 🔍 Phase III 4-arm open-label RCT; very high-risk localized PCa (≥2 of Gleason ≥8, T3-T4, PSA ≥20, N0M0); ADT 3 yrs; RT 78 Gy EQD2 prostate boost; primary EP cPFS

details.text: 🔗 WPRT vs prostate-only RT efficacy: POP-RT vs PEACE-2

details: ⚠️ Divergent results from POP-RT likely reflect modern PSMA PET/CT staging (filters occult nodal mets), longer ADT (3 vs 2 yrs), and higher RT dose — all attenuating incremental WPRT benefit
⚠️ <1/10 men died of PCa in first decade — challenges 'very high-risk' designation in PSMA-staged cohorts where occult mets are already excluded
❓ Whether WPRT benefits a biomarker-selected subset (high genomic risk, PSMA-occult nodal disease) remains unanswered

studies.name: PRIME
studies.tldr: PRIME interim (ESTRO 2026): no inferiority signal for 5-fx SBRT vs 25-fx moderate hypofractionation in high-risk/node-positive PCa; efficacy HR pending at 4-5yr.
details: 🔍 Phase III non-inferiority, N~434; high-risk/node-positive non-metastatic PCa; both arms: prostate + whole-pelvis RT + long-course ADT (~2yr); PSMA PET staging allowed
💊 Arm A: 36.25 Gy/5fx SBRT (every other day); Arm B: ~68 Gy/25fx moderate hypo (5fx/wk); pelvic nodes 25 Gy/5fx in both arms
📊 Primary BFFS (Phoenix nadir+2): early data show no inferiority signal for SBRT arm; no HR/CI/p reported yet; 4-5yr mature data awaited
🔗 vs HYPO-RT-PC (10-yr data): FFS HR 0.84 (95% CI 0.69-1.03), non-inferior; distinct population: node-negative only, no ADT, no pelvic RT
⚠️ Interim only: median f/u 1-2yr; no efficacy HR/CI; MFS and OS immature; non-inferiority not formally declared
❓ Whether SBRT non-inferiority holds at 4-5yr in node-positive disease with whole-pelvis RT is the pivotal unresolved question

open_questions: Does WPRT provide cPFS or MFS benefit in PSMA-staged, 3-yr ADT, high-dose RT cohorts, or has PEACE-2 closed that chapter for unselected pts?
Will PRIME confirm SBRT non-inferiority for whole-pelvis + prostate at 4-5yr in node-positive disease, where no prior SBRT RCT has operated?
What is the optimal ADT duration with MDT in oligorecurrent PCa, and does eugonadal MFS from RADIOSA translate to OS at longer follow-up?

sites.intro: Heavy fractionation and de-escalation day; OLIGOMA the standout with the first positive MDT RCT in the histology.

studies.name: OLIGOMA
studies.tldr: OLIGOMA: MDT + systemic therapy vs systemic therapy alone in oligometastatic breast cancer; mPFS 35.8 vs 20.4mo, HR 0.48 (0.25-0.91), p=0.021.
details: 🔍 Phase III RCT (ARO-2021-09, NCT04495309); N=87 (43 exp, 44 control); oligometastatic breast cancer any line, ≤5 metastatic lesions; randomized to systemic therapy ± local ablative RT to all lesions
📊 QoL co-primary endpoint met non-inferiority: EORTC QLQ-C30 summary score 72.2 vs 74.3 at 12 weeks; between-group difference -2.1 (95% CI -9.2 to 5.1); non-inferiority margin -10 pts
🔍 Cohort: majority ER/PR+ HER2- in 1L; >80% had 1-3 metastases; ~2/3 of lesions were bone; ~72% receiving 1L endocrine or chemo at enrollment
⚠️ Stopped early at <20% of original and <40% of amended target N; HR CI wide (0.25-0.91), reflecting small sample; OS data not reported; underpowered for subgroup analyses

🔗 First positive RCT for MDT in oligometastatic breast cancer specifically; adds to SABR-COMET, ORIOLE, and STOMP data in mixed/prostate oligomet settings; breast-specific evidence was largely retrospective until now
❓ Which subgroup benefits most (1L vs later lines, bone-dominant vs visceral, HR+ vs TNBC) remains open; mature OS and comprehensive subgroup analyses pending

studies.name: EORTC IM-MS (22922/10925)
studies.tldr: EORTC IM-MS at 20 years: IM-MS RT improves BCM but OS neutral (HR 1.00), counterbalanced by late cardiac and pulmonary toxicity.
details: 📊 Competing mortality at 20y (ITT): BCM reduced, non-BCM deaths increased
📊 DFS and DMFS at 20y (ITT): no significant difference
📊 pN0 subgroup at 20y: DFS 53.9% vs 53.6% (HR=0.93, p=0.318); DMFS 67.2% vs 67.4% (HR=0.93, p=0.397) — no benefit in node-negative pts

details.text: ⚠️ Late RT-related toxicity (absolute rates, IM-MS+ vs IM-MS-)

details: 🔗 DBCG IMN2 (Lancet Reg Health Eur 2024): modern IM-MS RT with mean heart doses 4-9x lower (MHD 1.2 Gy right-sided, 2.3 Gy left-sided) showed OS benefit at 15y in node-positive pts — era effect likely explains divergence from EORTC
❓ whether modern cardiac-sparing IM-MS RT (as in DBCG IMN2) avoids the non-BCM mortality offset seen here remains the key unanswered question

studies.name: IMPORT HIGH
studies.tldr: IMPORT HIGH 10-yr: 48Gy/15F SIB non-inferior to standard sequential boost (IBTR 3.7% vs 3.5%) with similar AEs; 53Gy SIB no added benefit.
details: 📊 10-yr IBTR rates (95% CI)
🔍 Phase III RCT, N=2617, 76 UK hospitals (2009-2015), pT1-3 pN0-N3a M0 BCS pts requiring tumour bed boost; 1:1:1 randomisation
📊 OS at 10 yrs: absolute difference vs 40Gy/15F = -0.5% (-3.0, 2.8) for 48Gy/15F and +1.5% (-1.4, 5.1) for 53Gy/15F — no OS detriment or benefit
💊 Late G3+ AEs at 10 yrs: breast distortion/shrinkage <18%, induration <10%, telangiectasia <2%, breast oedema <2% across all arms
🔗 5-yr IBTR from Lancet 2023 publication: 40+16Gy 1.9%, 48Gy 2.0%, 53Gy 3.2% — 10-yr data consistent with non-inferiority of 48Gy SIB holding over time
⚠️ 53Gy arm IBTR numerically higher at both 5 and 10 yrs — further dose escalation not beneficial and may be detrimental; no subgroup rescued this arm
❓ Whether SIB approach extends to nodal RT or post-mastectomy settings remains untested; long-term cardiac / second malignancy data not reported here

studies.name: DBCG HYPO
studies.tldr: DBCG HYPO 10-yr: 40Gy/15fr reduces grade 2-3 breast induration vs 50Gy/25fr (19.5% vs 24.7%, HR 0.76, p=0.005) with no difference in recurrence or survival.
details: 📊 1° EP (3-yr G≥2 breast induration): 10-yr update shows 19.5% (40Gy) vs 24.7% (50Gy); HR 0.76 (95% CI 0.62-0.92), p=0.005
🔍 Phase III RCT (1:1), N=1,882 women with node-negative breast cancer or DCIS; enrolled Denmark/Norway/Germany 2009-2014; median f/u 12.8 yrs
📊 10-yr OS: 93.0% (40Gy) vs 92.1% (50Gy); HR 0.81 (95% CI 0.63-1.04), p=0.10 — no significant difference
No significant differences in locoregional recurrence, distant failure, or breast cancer mortality between arms

🔗 Consistent with START-A/B (Lancet Oncol 2013) and FAST-Forward (Lancet 2020) showing non-inferior tumor control with hypofractionation; DBCG HYPO adds 10-yr induration data in a prospectively randomized Nordic/German cohort
⚠️ Primary EP was 3-yr induration (morbidity), not oncologic outcome — long-term recurrence/survival comparisons are secondary analyses; trial not powered for OS differences
❓ Ultra-hypofractionation (FAST-Forward 26Gy/5fr) now increasingly adopted — unclear where 40Gy/15fr fits vs 5-fraction schedules in current practice

studies.name: HypoG-01
studies.tldr: HypoG-01 patterns-of-failure analysis: LRR infrequent (20/118 events), failure patterns comparable between 40 Gy/15 fx and 50 Gy/25 fx arms.
details: 🔍 Pre-planned secondary analysis of phase III RCT; N=1,260, median f/u 4.8 yrs; randomized 3-week (40 Gy/15 fx) vs 5-week (50 Gy/25 fx) RT + tumour-bed boost; ITT population

subdetails: IDR: 61 events (isolated distant recurrence, 51% of events)

details: 📊 Site of iLRR: 20/30 sites in-volume (67%); 19/30 nodal sites, mainly L1 & L2 levels
📊 Patterns of failure not obviously different between 3-week and 5-week arms
🔍 LRR classified per ESTRO CTV guidelines: in-volume (within CTV), marginal (outside CTV, ≥50% prescribed dose), out-of-volume (<50% prescribed dose)
🔗 Majority of LRR within treated volumes supports adequacy of ESTRO-guided contouring for moderate hypofractionation schedules
⚠️ Secondary/patterns-of-failure analysis only; no HR/effect size for LRR rate difference between arms reported in source tweets — comparative LRR rates by arm not extractable from available data
studies.key_figure_caption: 118 first oncological events: iLRR 19, cLRR 1, IDR 61, second malignancy 37; LRR 16%, IDR 51%, second malignancy 31%. iLRR sites: 20/30 in-volume (67%), 19/30 nodal.

studies.name: APBI-IMRT Florence
studies.tldr: APBI-IMRT Florence: 15-yr IBTR 7.7% vs 4.2% (HR 1.57, p=0.17) — no difference in LRC, DM, or OS.
details: 🔍 Phase III, N=520, 1:1 APBI (30Gy/5#) vs WBI (50Gy/25# + 10Gy TBB); eligibility: BCS, pT<25mm, FSM ≥5mm, age >40; accrual 2005-2013; NCT02104895
📊 15-yr IBTR: APBI 7.7% vs WBI 4.2%, HR 1.57 (95% CI 0.82-3.04), p=0.17 — numerical excess not statistically significant

⚠️ IBTR excess driven by new ipsilateral primaries (APBI 5.9% vs WBI 2.7%, p=0.09), not true local relapses (2.1% vs 1.6%, p=0.75) — raises question of field effect or at-risk parenchyma outside PBI field

🔗 Consistent with IMPORT/RAPID/GEC-ESTRO: no OS or LRC detriment at long f/u for APBI in selected low-risk pts; IBTR signal magnitude similar to RAPID (8.8% vs 3.0% at 8yr) though p not significant here

⚠️ Single-institution Florence trial; per-protocol analysis used for toxicity/cosmesis, ITT for survival — protocol-specified equivalence margin was 5% at 5yr; 15-yr IBTR delta of 3.5% approaches but does not cross that threshold
❓ Whether IBTR excess (new primaries specifically) translates to meaningful survival impact at 20yr remains open; contralateral breast event rate numerically higher in WBI arm (unexplained)

studies.name: Dutch Breast Boost Indication Study
studies.tldr: Dutch registry: 10-yr IBTR <3% for 0-2 risk factors with or without boost, suggesting boost omission feasible in low-risk pts.
details: 🔍 Retrospective Dutch registry, BCT pts 2012-2016, N~30,000+; primary outcome cumulative incidence of IBTR stratified by risk-factor count (0-2 vs ≥3 vs uncertain)

details.text: 📊 10-yr IBTR by risk-factor group and boost assignment

details: 📐 Assisi thresholds used as decision anchors: <3% at 10 yr = omit boost safe; <6% at 10 yr without boost = omit acceptable

🔗 vs EORTC 22881/10882 (Bartelink, Lancet Oncol 2015): boost reduced IBTR ~50% in that era; current registry rates far lower, consistent with systemic therapy intensification narrowing absolute benefit

⚠️ retrospective registry design; boost allocation not randomized — higher-risk pts selectively boosted (≥3 RF group: 733 boosted vs 149 not), limiting causal inference; residual confounding by indication substantial
⚠️ ≥3 RF subgroup small in no-boost arm (N=149); 10-yr rate 2.7% doesn't cross 3% threshold, but CI not reported — underpowered to rule out clinically meaningful excess
❓ whether specific RF combinations (e.g., TN + no pCR vs age <40 alone) drive remaining boost benefit unresolved; further subgroup work needed

studies.name: RAPCHEM
studies.tldr: RAPCHEM 10-yr data: response-adapted locoregional RT de-escalation post-neoadjuvant yields recurrence rate <3%.
details: 📊 10-yr locoregional recurrence <3% with nodal-response-guided RT de-escalation after neoadjuvant chemotherapy
🔍 response-adapted strategy: RT field/dose downscaled based on nodal response to neoadjuvant; full RT omission not yet validated in this cohort
🔗 complete RT omission remains investigational -- ongoing prospective trials + B-51 still accruing/maturing
⚠️ single tweet summary from ESTRO 2026; no breakdown by receptor subtype, nodal burden, or pCR status reported in source

studies.name: DBCG RT Natural
studies.tldr: DBCG RT Natural: 4-yr LR 1.5% with PBI vs 9.8% without RT (randomized) in low-risk ≥60yr breast cancer; trial stopped early.
details: 🔍 phase III, N≈926 planned; ≥60yr, pT1N0, grade 1-2, ER+ ≥10%, HER2-normal, unifocal, breast conservation, margin ≥2mm; PBI 40Gy/15fr vs no PBI; ET per guideline (recommended for pT1c and/or grade 2); median f/u 4yr
📊 LR by RT + ET (4-yr CIF) — 2×2 breakdown — comparison values omitted (cell number "1.3" not verified in source)
⚠️ median f/u 4yr vs 5-yr primary EP; stopping rules invoked at interim — longer f/u may narrow the absolute LR gap
⚠️ S-RT 'Natural' arm is self-selecting, not randomized — limits inference from that cohort; however S-RT LR (8.2%) tracks closely with randomized -RT (9.8%), suggesting limited confounding
🔗 vs PRIME II (NEJM 2015, ≥65yr ER+ low-risk on ET): 5-yr LR 4.1% no-RT vs 1.3% RT — DBCG gap larger, consistent with lower ET adherence in -RT arm here
❓ whether 5-fraction PBI would perform equivalently; ET adherence in the -RT arm is the dominant confounder for the surgery-only vs combined-modality comparison

open_questions: Which OLIGOMA subgroups drive PFS benefit: ER/PR+ vs TNBC, 1L vs later lines, bone-dominant vs visceral, 1-3 vs 4-5 lesions?
Does modern cardiac-sparing IM-MS RT (MHD 1-2 Gy, DBCG IMN2 era) avoid the non-BCM mortality offset that neutralised OS in EORTC at 20yr?
Have systemic therapy advances already eroded the absolute boost benefit below clinical detectability in low-risk BCT, rendering boost omission safe for most pts with 0-2 risk factors?

sites.intro: OligoCare (N=2447) is now the largest prospective SBRT registry for oligometastatic disease; CRC radioresistance emerges as a distinct biological problem.

studies.name: EXTEND
studies.tldr: EXTEND phase II RCT: MDT added to SOC for oligometastatic solid tumors; primary aggregated multi-histology analysis published JCO 2026.
details: 🔍 Phase II randomized trial; multi-histology basket design; MDT (SBRT/EBRT) + SOC vs SOC alone across oligometastatic solid tumors
📊 No effect size reported in source tweets; primary aggregated analysis published JCO May 2026 (DOI 10.1200/JCO-25-02856)
🔬 ctDNA correlatives presented synchronously at ESTRO26 -- may link MDT benefit to molecular response; data not in source tweets
⚠️ Basket design aggregates histologies; pooled analysis can obscure heterogeneous benefit across tumor types -- per-histology subgroup data needed for clinical translation
🔗 Aligns with SABR-COMET (Lancet 2019) and ORIOLE (JAMA Oncol 2020) showing PFS/OS signals with MDT in oligomets, though those were earlier, smaller, or more restricted histology
❓ Per-basket (histology-specific) HRs, ctDNA clearance as predictive biomarker, and OS maturity not reported in source tweets

studies.name: OligoCare
studies.tldr: OligoCare (EORTC registry, N=2447): SBRT 3yr local control 88.6%; CRC worst at 19.6% 3yr local failure vs 8.1% prostate.
details: 📊 Overall local in-field failure: 5.0% at 1yr, 11.4% at 3yr (99% CI 10.0-12.9%); 2447 pts, 237 events, median f/u 31 months
🔍 EORTC OligoCare prospective registry, 57 institutions, July 2019-July 2025; 3533 lesions; primary sites: prostate 42%, NSCLC 22%, colorectal 21%, breast 15%
📐 Minimum PTV dose: most critical technical determinant of local control per multivariable analysis; dose adequacy predicts outcome more than histology in non-CRC pts
📊 De novo OMD showed better local control than repeat OMD, attributed to higher achievable delivered dose in treatment-naive oligomet sites

⚠️ CRC local failure 19.6% at 3yr despite receiving highest median dose-per-fraction of any tumor type -- suggests intrinsic radioresistance, not underdosing; combination strategies or dose escalation warranted
🔗 Largest prospective OMD SBRT registry to date; complements RCT signal from SABR-COMET and ORIOLE with real-world 57-center generalizability

open_questions: Does dose escalation beyond current constraints or systemic combination (immunotherapy, chemotherapy) overcome CRC's 19.6% 3yr local failure despite highest delivered dose-per-fraction in OligoCare?
In EXTEND, which histologies drove the pooled PFS signal, and does ctDNA clearance prospectively identify MDT responders across tumor types?

studies.name: RCC SBRT 5-year Local Control
studies.tldr: Single-institution SBRT series reports 100% local control at 5 years for renal cell carcinoma.
details: 📊 1° EP: 100% local control at 5 years — no effect size beyond this reported in source tweets
🔍 trial design, N, fractionation schema, and eligibility criteria not reported in source tweets
⚠️ single tweet with no image or data table; no denominator, f/u distribution, or competing-risk context available
⚠️ 100% LC at 5y would be exceptional — selection bias (small primaries, favorable histology) and short median f/u could inflate apparent control rates in retrospective/single-arm series
🔗 benchmark: IROCK consortium and prospective TROG 15.03 data suggest 5y LC ~85-95% depending on dose/fractionation and tumor size — 100% would sit at upper bound of published ranges
❓ whether LC translates to OS or cancer-specific survival benefit vs surveillance or thermal ablation in T1a-b RCC remains unresolved

studies.name: DIREKHT
studies.tldr: DIREKHT: post-op RT volume/dose de-escalation in HNSCC (contralateral neck sparing and/or primary CTV 56 Gy); no effect size reported in source tweets.
details: 🔍 post-operative RT de-escalation trial in HNSCC; interventions: contralateral neck sparing and/or primary CTV dose reduction to 56 Gy in selected pts
📊 no effect size, endpoint results, or p-values reported in source tweets
❓ eligibility criteria for de-escalation selection (contralateral neck sparing vs dose reduction) not specified in source tweet
🔗 de-escalation theme consistent with growing evidence base (e.g., MC0432, ECOG-ACRIN 3311) seeking to reduce long-term xerostomia/dysphagia in low-risk HNSCC subsets
⚠️ single tweet with no data — full results, primary endpoint, and trial design not determinable from source

Il randomized trial

ESTRO
2026
0000g
Background
Metastasis-free survival (MFS) has been validated as a surrogate endpoint for overall survival in non-metastatic prostate
cancer, and may represent an adaptable and clinically meaningful endpoint in oligorecurrent disease.
The phase II RADIOSA trial compared stereotactic body radiotherapy (SBRT) alone (Arm A) versus SBRT plus 6-month
androgen deprivation therapy (ADT, Arm B). This post-hoe analysis evaluated MFS and eugonadal MFS after
testosterone recovery, with a visual mapping of disease trajectories through a Sankey diagram.
Methods
A total of 102 patients were randomized 1:l to Arm A (SBRT) or Arm B (SBRT + ADT).
Median follow-up (Reverse KM) was 49.23 months (95% C1 42.47-54.8).
MFS was defined as the time between randomisation and the appearance of a metastatic recurrence (any MI) on imaging.
Eugonadal MFS was calculated from testosterone recovery to new metastasis or last follow-up.
Kaplan-Meier (KM) survival curves were compared with log-rank tests; hazard ratios were estimated using Cox models.

Study aim
Given that a boost reduces the risk of IBTR by approximately 50%' and guided by the Assisi
thresholds?, the objective of our study is:
To determine 5-and 10-year IBTR rates in patients treated with or without a radiotherapy boost
To identify subgroups of patients in whom a boost may be omitted, i.e.
• Treated with a boost who have 10-year IBTR rates of <3%
• Treated without a boost who have 10-year IBTR rates of <6%

of risk factors

Conclusions
• Low IBTR rates across all patient subgroups
• Based on the presence of combined risk factors, 5-and 10-year IBTR rates exceeded
the Assisi thresholds only in:
• Patients with ≥ 3 risk factors treated with a boost at 10 years
Take home messages
• The low IBTR rates across all subgroups suggest that
• In patients with 0-2 risk factors a tumour bed boost can be omitted, in the modern
systemic therapy era
• In patients with ≥ 3 risk factors the clinical relevance of a tumour bed boost remains
open to discussion
• Further research is needed to identify specific subgroups of patients who benefit from a
boost

initial RT-plan CT scan

Background & Methods
Background
Moderately hypofractionated adjuvant whole-breast radiotherapy for early
node-negative breast cancer is standard worldwide, but long-term
randomised evidence remains limited. Here, we present the Danish
Breast Cancer Group HYPO trial prespecified 10-year outcomes.
Methods
• Phase III randomised non-inferiority trial (1:1) across Denmark,
Norway and Germany (2009-2014)
• 1,882 women with node-negative breast cancer or DCIS
• Whole-breast RT: 50 Gy/25 fractions vs 40 Gy/15 fractions
• Primary endpoint: 3-year grade 22 breast induration at two consequtive
visits, or at final follow-up
• Morbidity evaluated year 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 10
• These results are the prespecified 10-year analyses of toxicity.

Conclusion & Take-Home Message
40 Gy / 15 fractions reduced long-term breast
induration without compromising recurrence or
survival outcomes.
At 12.8 years follow-up, results showed:
• Lower fibrosis risk with maintained cancer outcomes
• Supports continued use of moderately hypofractionated
whole-breast radiotherapy
Less toxicity. Same cancer

2026
At 15 years median of follow up, the phase III APBI-IMRT Florence trial (NCT02104895)
demonstrates durable oncological outcomes with PB in selected patients with early breast cancer
Although a numerical increase in IBTR was observed, this was largely attributable to new
primary tumours rather than true local recurrences.
No differences were observed in loco-regional control, distant metastasis, breast cancer-
specific survival or overall survival.
These findings provide mature evidence supporting PBl as a safe de-escalation strategy and
reinforce its role as a standard treatment option in appropriately selected patients

• High quality trial; shows importance of independent data monitoring & planned
stopping rules
• RT + ET gives optimal local control
• RT or ET appear to have similar local control in low-risk group
• Surgery only is associated with high local recurrence rates even in low-risk group

SUMMARY
• POP-RT (2 years ADT) demonstrated significant benefit in bFFS, cFFS, and MFS with WPRT.
• PEACE-2 (3 years ADT, modern imaging, dose-escalated RT) showed no significant improvement in bPFS, MFS, CSS, or OS.
Only a borderline trend in cPFS (HR 0.81, p=0.09). No additional toxicity observed.
